title: Unnecessary character escapes should be removed
description: Unnecessary character escapes should be removed. The backslash (\) character indicates that the next character should be treated as a literal character rather than as a special character or string delimiter. For instance, it is common to escape single quotes inside a string literal like 'It\'s a beautiful day'. However, escaping is only meaningful for special characters like quotes, newlines, tabs, backslashes, and unicode/hex sequences. Escaping regular letters that have no special meaning (like \w, \o, \a, \d, \c, \e, \g, \i, \j, \k, \l, \m, \p, \q, \s, \y in regular strings) is unnecessary and should be removed. Note: \w, \d, \s etc. ARE valid in regex patterns where they have special meaning.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.
